summary_one_line: Input must begin a hex character reference '&#x' so the third byte after '&#' equals 'x' (0x78).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A character-reference prefix where RAW=='&', NXT(1)=='#', and NXT(2)=='x' (the literal byte 0x78), i.e. the '&#x' hex-charref opener rather than the '&#' decimal form.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolved the true branch in all 10 trials; the per-role overlay shows the winner taking the hex path (lines 2318-2344 marked [W]) and hitting line 2317 true (T=12) while line 2349 (the decimal else-if) is never taken by W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocked 9/10; its inputs reach RAW=='&' and NXT(1)=='#' (line 2316 L T=84) but the third byte is not 'x', so it falls through to the decimal '&#' else-if at line 2349 (L T=84) and stays on the false side of line 2317 (L T=0 F=84).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer CMP for NXT(2)=='x' and writes a Hamming-distance bucket into CMP_MAP; an input whose third byte is closer to 0x78 adds a new bucket and is retained, giving a gradient that drives the byte toward 'x'. naive has no CMP introspection and only edge-counter feedback, so it can only land 'x' by lucky havoc byte mutation, explaining 1/10.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the single-byte equality comparison NXT(2)=='x' at parser.c:2317, whose true side is globally blocked.
The true (hex) side of line 2317 is the globally blocked side.
value_profile resolved this branch in all 10 trials while naive blocked in 9 of 10.
The loser naive resolves only 1/10 and blocks 9/10 at this branch.
The winner takes the hex-charref body (SKIP(3); GROW; hex loop) while the loser takes the decimal else-if path.
Per-role branch counts show the winner takes line 2317 true (T=12) and the loser only false (F=84), with the loser instead taking the decimal else-if at 2349.
value_profile's mechanism is integer-CMP interception writing a coarse operand-distance bucket into CMP_MAP that is preserved as new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance
bucket)

evidence_trail.claim: An input producing a CMP-operand pair closer to matching adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is kept in corpus, providing the gradient toward 'x'.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only edge-counter feedback and no CMP introspection, so it cannot gradient-solve the byte comparison.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so lineage dash-row verification is not required. The MECHANISM CONTEXT section confirms value_profile uses CMP_MAP operand-distance buckets and explicitly states value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, so an I2S-floor check would not apply here.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a single-byte equality NXT(2)=='x' where value_profile shows no resolution advantage over naive (both resolve at the same rate), indicating the CMP_MAP distance gradient gives no edge on a 1-byte comparand.

weakest_evidence_point: No winner/loser seed in BRANCH SEEDS visibly contains the literal '&#x' opener at a fixed offset, so the byte-level proof that the winner satisfies NXT(2)=='x' rests on the per-role branch counts rather than direct seed bytes.
